campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council held a March 24, 2026 meeting that opened with tributes to K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who recently passed away. The council also approved an updated beekeeping ordinance, discussed a state bill about development near transit areas (with some council members sitting out due to conflicts of interest), and reviewed a study recommending a regional task force to address homelessness in West Valley.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial and police dog program
Updated beekeeping rules approved
State transit-oriented development bill (SB 79) — three council members recused
West Valley homeless services task force recommendation
City's 2025 financial audit accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a special meeting to meet with five different city commissions and agree on their priorities and plans for the next fiscal year. The commissions covered topics like preserving historic buildings, running the library and public art programs, managing parks and recreation, improving traffic safety, and long-term city planning. The council also discussed one pending lawsuit in a closed session.
keyTopics: Historic preservation priorities for 2026-27
Library and public art program plans
Parks and recreation priorities
Traffic safety initiatives
City planning focus areas

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to handle routine administrative matters including approving meeting minutes and financial reports. The council also adopted policies on weed removal in the city, affordable housing unit transfers, and approved raising two new flags (Progress Pride and Juneteenth). They also heard a presentation from the regional transit authority and discussed their housing plans for the coming year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion and final report
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
New flags approved for City Hall (Progress Pride and Juneteenth)
Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority presentation
Housing element implementation schedule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve various official documents and reports from earlier meetings. Key items included reviewing the city's finances for January, approving a new housing development project (Solana), and confirming plans for community health and senior services in the coming year.
keyTopics: Approved housing development (Solana Project/Tract 10657)
Reviewed city finances and investments for January 2026
Assessed progress on housing and general city planning goals
Confirmed 2026 community health and senior services work plan

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize their upcoming agendas. They reviewed and finalized the items that will be discussed at the April 7 meeting, and looked ahead at what might be added to the April 14 meeting. The council also reviewed official records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for April 7 City Council meeting
Planning draft agenda for April 14 meeting
Setting specific times for certain agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewing public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments. The meeting included reports from various city committees and discussed upcoming agendas for local boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff updates
Upcoming meetings for city boards and commissions
Meeting materials available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that funds affordable housing and community support programs in Sunnyvale. The group approved meeting notes from their previous session and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to help residents with housing and social services.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for affordable housing programs
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding decisions
Social services and human services funding requests
Commission meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council discussed several initiatives to support local businesses and encourage shopping in Cupertino. The main focus was on helping business owners comply with licensing requirements, promoting local shops through summer campaigns, and offering financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to the area.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help non-compliant businesses get licensed
Outreach efforts to shopping centers and local business owners
Summer promotional campaign encouraging residents to shop locally
City-sponsored events through partnerships with private companies
Financial incentives and support for new retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city discussed matters related to historic buildings and landmarks in the community. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom to observe and comment on the proceedings.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission via email by noon on meeting day
Remote participation via Zoom or phone call

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative housekeeping tasks, including approving meeting minutes and scheduling calendars. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to an annual wellness conference and confirmed a new representative to serve on the Economic Development board for the school district.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming City Council meeting schedule
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual Conference
New Milpitas Unified School District representative appointed to Economic Development committee
City Manager given authority to negotiate and execute agreements (details cut off)

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main items: an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and a contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for the city for three years at about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report addition to agenda
